demographics.living_situation: 3-week-old infant living at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — his hands curled inward, his face started twitching non-stop, and he is extremely jittery when we touch him. He's only 3 weeks old and he has a heart condition.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started suddenly about an hour ago and it won't stop even when we gently hold his arms — we're really scared something is seriously wrong.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on abruptly about an hour ago
symptom_narrative.duration: one episode already lasted around 20 minutes
symptom_narrative.character: his hands curl inward, his face twitches non-stop, and he goes very jittery and stiff
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst we've seen — it doesn't stop when we hold his arms still
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mostly his hands and his face
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came in a sustained episode; he seems set off or worse when we touch or handle him
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing we tried helped — holding his limbs still doesn't stop it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: touching or handling him seems to set it off
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he's been generally grizzly and feeding poorly since he came home from the special care nursery; during one spasm his lips looked a bit blue for a moment while he seemed to hold his breath
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing this severe before, but he's been unsettled and feeding poorly since discharge
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he's a newborn and completely dependent on us; he's medically fragile because of his heart and palate

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a heart condition he was born with (they told us it's Tetralogy of Fallot) that hasn't been fixed yet
past_medical_history.how_managed: on heart medicines; followed by the cardiac team

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a cleft in the roof of his mouth (cleft palate)
past_medical_history.how_managed: under the care of the cleft/feeding team, not yet repaired

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: he spent time in the special care nursery after he was born

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: his heart medicines (we have the charts)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: first-time parents feeling overwhelmed managing his complex medication charts; both parents at home with him now

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: born with a heart defect and cleft palate; time in special care nursery; unsettled and feeding poorly since discharge

objective_data_offered.value: hands curling inward at the wrists and knuckles with the fingers held out straight; face and cheek twitching
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent-held phone video of a moving, distressed newborn — quality and interpretation limited

objective_data_offered.value: severe jitteriness that keeps going even when we hold his arms still; he seems extra jumpy when touched
his lips looked a little blue for a few seconds during one spasm while he seemed to hold his breath, then went back to normal
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: brief, self-resolved colour change reported by a frightened parent; not a measured oxygen reading

objective_data_offered.value: the parents note he has a small jaw and low-set ears
